channel.title: RTL on EasyFPGA
channel.description: Recent content in RTL on EasyFPGA

item.title: Custom RTL Design: TX & RX
item.description: Implementing UART TX and RX entirely in SystemVerilog: baud rate generator design, TX FSM, center-sampling RX FSM, and loopback testbench strategy.

item.title: UART Implementation Methods
item.description: Three practical ways to build UART on FPGA: vendor IP, soft processor plus UART IP, and custom RTL, with trade-offs in flexibility, performance, and development effort.

item.title: RS-232 and RS-485 for UART Systems
item.description: How UART logic maps to physical layers: RS-232 versus RS-485 wiring, voltage levels, distance, noise tolerance, and deployment trade-offs.

item.title: UART Frame Format
item.description: A practical guide to UART frame fields: start/data/parity/stop bits, LSB-first ordering, overhead, and throughput implications.

item.title: What is UART?
item.description: UART fundamentals for FPGA engineers: asynchronous communication basics, frame structure intuition, baud rate, clock drift, and practical use cases.

item.title: CRC Calculator & RTL Generator: Complete Guide
item.description:
CRC (Cyclic Redundancy Check) is the most widely used error-detection mechanism in digital communication — from Ethernet frames to USB packets to SSD data integrity. This post explains how CRC works mathematically and shows how to use the free online CRC Calculator & RTL Generator to produce ready-to-simulate SystemVerilog code for your FPGA design.

What Is CRC?

CRC appends a checksum to a data block so the receiver can detect corruption. The transmitter computes a CRC value over the data and appends it; the receiver recomputes the CRC and compares. A mismatch means a transmission error occurred.

item.title: Refactor, Refine, Fix, and Optimize: Precise Terminology for RTL Development
item.description: Using the right word when reviewing or requesting RTL changes makes communication faster and less ambiguous. Here is what each term means in an FPGA context.

item.title: Ethernet II + IPv4 + UDP Frame Structure Reference
item.description: A complete byte-level breakdown of an Ethernet II / IPv4 / UDP frame — the essential reference for FPGA-based network packet processing.
